examples.title: Algorithm: Linear Search
examples.description: Finds an element in an unsorted array by checking each element sequentially
examples.code:
ALGORITHM LinearSearch(arr, target)
INPUT: arr (array of elements), target (element to find)
OUTPUT: index of target if found, -1 otherwise

BEGIN
 FOR i FROM 0 TO arr.length - 1
 IF arr[i] == target THEN
 RETURN i
 END FOR
 RETURN -1
END
examples.explanation: Iterates through the array, checking each element for a match. If found, returns the index. If not found, returns -1.

examples.title: Algorithm: Bubble Sort
examples.description: Sorts an array by repeatedly swapping adjacent elements if they are in the wrong order
examples.code:
ALGORITHM BubbleSort(arr)
INPUT: arr (array of elements)
OUTPUT: sorted array

BEGIN
 FOR i FROM 0 TO arr.length - 1
 FOR j FROM 0 TO arr.length - i - 1
 IF arr[j] > arr[j + 1] THEN
 SWAP arr[j] AND arr[j + 1]
 END FOR
 RETURN arr
END
examples.explanation: Repeatedly iterates through the array, swapping adjacent elements if they are in the wrong order. Continues until the array is sorted.
